title: Parkio Daily Briefing for April 30, 2026
teaser: New Star Wars Day merch revealed, plus Disney teams with Make-A-Wish for epic Avengers Campus event.

rides.name: Guardians of the Galaxy: Mission BREAKOUT
rides.note: Featured in recent Make-A-Wish event with Anthony Mackie

rides.name: WEB SLINGERS: A Spider-Man Adventure
rides.note: Popular with content creators during Wishes Assemble

bignews.title: Disney and Make-A-Wish Grant Nearly 40 Avengers-Inspired Wishes at California Adventure
bignews.body: Disney partnered with Make-A-Wish and MrBeast for Wishes Assemble, a first-of-its-kind event at Avengers Campus. Nearly 40 kids battling critical illnesses joined content creators including Mark Rober, Dude Perfect, and PrestonPlayz for Super Hero-themed activities. Anthony Mackie surprised wish kids with Captain America action figures and rode Guardians of the Galaxy – Mission: BREAKOUT with 9-year-old Coen. The event included comic book art lessons, Beast Games, science experiments, and a private evening celebration where Paul Bettany made a surprise appearance. Bob Iger received the inaugural WishMaker Lifetime Achievement Award for helping grant over 110,000 wishes during his tenure.

topstories.title: Star Wars Day Merch Revealed for Disney Store and Parks
topstories.body: Disney unveiled new Star Wars Day merchandise launching May 4, 2026, on DisneyStore.com at 8 a.m. PST. The collection includes a Darth Maul ear headband from BaubleBar featuring his iconic red and black coloring in rhinestone form, plus a Darth Maul-inspired double-sleeve tee from Her Universe exclusive to Disney Store. Daily reveals will continue through May 3 showcasing more products arriving for Star Wars Day.

topstories.title: Virtual Queues Required for May the 4th Merch Events at Disney's Hollywood Studios and Disney Springs
topstories.body: Walt Disney World will implement virtual queues for May the 4th merchandise events on Monday, May 4, 2026. Guests can join the Disney's Hollywood Studios virtual queue at 7 a.m. ET (park admission and reservation required) and the Disney Springs queue at 9 a.m. ET via the My Disney Experience app. Merchandise mobile checkout will be available at the Hollywood Studios event for seamless shopping.

spotlight.title: Disney's 45-Year Partnership with Make-A-Wish Continues to Shine
spotlight.body: Disney's wish-granting legacy began at Disneyland Resort over 45 years ago and has grown to help grant more than 175,000 wishes with Make-A-Wish—a Disney wish is granted every hour of every day. Last year alone, Disney provided $30 million in support to the organization. The recent Wishes Assemble event showcased the power of this partnership, bringing together cast members, content creators, and licensees including Hasbro, LEGO, Funko, and others to create magical moments for kids battling critical illnesses. Visit Wish.org/Disney to learn how you can help make wishes come true.
